title: IndexChangeRecipe
description:
A SEPAL Index Change recipe. Compares the same single-band index between two source images (a 'from' and a 'to' image) and produces difference / ratio / normalized-difference bands plus an optional categorical change map.

**What this recipe is for.** Detect change in any continuous spectral index (NDVI, NBR, NDMI, etc.) between two snapshots of the same area. Typical use cases: deforestation (NDVI drop), burn severity (dNBR), water-body change (NDWI shift), urbanization (built-up index). The recipe is index-agnostic — it operates on whichever single band you choose from the input images.

**Pipeline.** (1) Pull the chosen `band` from each of `fromImage` and `toImage`. (2) Compute three difference bands: `difference = to − from`, `normalized_difference = (to − from) / (to + from)`, `ratio = to / from`. (3) If both images carry an `errorBand`, propagate per-pixel errors as `error = sqrt(fromError² + toError²)` and a `confidence = |difference| / error` band. (4) If `legend.entries` is non-empty, materialize a categorical `change` band: each pixel is assigned the legend value of the FIRST matching entry (entries are evaluated bottom-up — the LAST matching entry wins per `ee.mosaic` semantics). When `confidence ≤ options.minConfidence`, `difference` is forced to 0 in the change-band evaluation, so low-confidence pixels fall into a 'no change' bucket.

**AOI semantics.** No separate AOI field — the AOI is the intersection of the geometries of `fromImage` and `toImage`.

This schema describes the persisted `model` of an *initialized* recipe. Cross-field constraints that JSON Schema cannot express are enforced by the companion rules.

dates.description: Optional cosmetic record of the from/to dates the user is mosaicking. The EE backend does NOT read this field — date semantics are entirely captured by the underlying `fromImage` and `toImage` recipes/assets. Included for round-trip compatibility with the GUI's default model. The LLM does not need to set or update it.

inputImage.title: IndexChangeInputImage
inputImage.description: A single source image — either a SEPAL recipe reference or an Earth Engine asset id — together with the band the recipe should treat as the index, and an optional uncertainty band.

type.description: What `id` refers to. `RECIPE_REF` — id of another SEPAL recipe whose image output is consumed. `ASSET` — Earth Engine Image / ImageCollection asset id.

id.description: SEPAL recipe id (when type=RECIPE_REF) or Earth Engine asset id (when type=ASSET).

bands.description: All bands the source exposes — populated by the GUI on selection. The recipe only uses `band` (and optionally `errorBand`); `bands` is informational.

band.description: Single band of the source to use as the index. Both `fromImage.band` and `toImage.band` must reference comparable values — typically the same band name (e.g. both `ndvi`).

errorBand.description: Optional per-pixel uncertainty band. When present on BOTH `fromImage` and `toImage`, the recipe emits an additional `error` band (`sqrt(fromError² + toError²)`) and a `confidence` band (`|difference| / error`). When absent on either, `error` and `confidence` are not produced and `options.minConfidence` is ignored.

bandMin.description: Optional display-time minimum for the chosen band, used for visualization scaling in the GUI. Has no effect on processing.

bandMax.description: Optional display-time maximum for the chosen band, used for visualization scaling in the GUI. Has no effect on processing.

errorBandMax.description: Optional display-time maximum for the error band. Has no effect on processing.

legend.title: IndexChangeLegend
legend.description:
Categorical change classes. When `entries` is non-empty, the recipe produces a `change` band whose value at each pixel is the legend value of the LAST entry whose `constraints` are satisfied. (The backend reverses the entries and uses `ee.mosaic`, so later entries override earlier ones.)

Leaving `entries: []` (or omitting `legend`) suppresses the `change` band; the recipe then only produces difference / ratio / normalized_difference (plus error/confidence when error bands are present).

The defaults define three classes — Decrease (red), Stable (white), Increase (green) — keyed off the sign of `difference`.

id.description: UUID for stable React keys; not used by the backend.

value.description: Integer class id stored in the output `change` band. Must be unique across entries.

label.description: Human-readable class name shown in the legend and visualizations.

color.description: Hex color (`#RRGGBB`) used for visualizing this class.

booleanOperator.description:
How the entry's `constraints` are combined.

- `'and'` (default) — all constraints must be satisfied. The recipe takes the per-pixel min over each constraint mask.
- `'or'` — any constraint suffices. The recipe takes the per-pixel max over each constraint mask.

Ignored when `constraints` has 0 or 1 entries.

constraints.description: Constraint clauses combined per `booleanOperator`. An empty array masks the whole image for this entry — typically not what you want.

constraint.title: IndexChangeConstraint
constraint.description:
A single comparison applied to one of the recipe's intermediate bands (`difference`, `normalized_difference`, `ratio`). The shape varies by operator:

- For comparison operators (`<`, `≤`, `>`, `≥`, `=`): use `value`.
- For `range`: use `from`, `to`, `fromInclusive`, `toInclusive`.

Note the unicode `≤` / `≥` (U+2264 / U+2265) — these are the actual operator strings the backend expects, NOT `<=` / `>=`.

description.description: Human-readable summary of the comparison shown in the GUI; not used by the backend.

image.description: Constraints in indexChange always refer to bands of this recipe itself. Must be the literal string `'this-recipe'`.

band.description: Which intermediate band the constraint compares against.

operator.description: Comparison operator. `≤` and `≥` are the unicode characters U+2264 / U+2265 — the JSON form for the LLM is `\u2264` / `\u2265`. `range` selects pixels whose value lies between `from` and `to`.

value.description: Right-hand-side value for comparison operators (`<`, `≤`, `>`, `≥`, `=`). Required for those operators; ignored for `range`.

from.description: Lower bound for `range`. Required for `range` operator only.

to.description: Upper bound for `range`. Required for `range` operator only.

fromInclusive.description: Whether the lower bound is inclusive. Required for `range` operator only.

toInclusive.description: Whether the upper bound is inclusive. Required for `range` operator only.

allOf.description: Range operator requires from, to, fromInclusive, toInclusive.

minConfidence.description:
Minimum `confidence = |difference| / error` for a pixel to be considered changed. Pixels with `confidence ≤ minConfidence` have their `difference` clamped to 0 in the change-band evaluation, which routes them to whichever legend entry matches `difference = 0` (typically a 'Stable' bucket).

Default `2.5` — roughly equivalent to a 2.5σ change. Higher values are more conservative (fewer change pixels, lower false-positive rate); lower values catch more change at the cost of noise.

Valid range [0, 20]; the GUI snaps to typical values 0, 1, 2, 3, 5, 10, 20 on a log slider. **Only meaningful when both `fromImage.errorBand` AND `toImage.errorBand` are set** — otherwise the `confidence` band isn't produced and this field is silently ignored.
